description: An HCI researcher is analyzing how quickly users can process multi-level menus using Choice Reaction Time (CRT) data from ~/pebl/data/hicks_law_data.csv. Clean and model the data using Hick's Law (RT = Intercept + Slope * log2(N)). Participants with an overall accuracy across all trials of less than 60% are excluded. For the remaining data, only correct trials (correct == 1) with reaction times between 150 ms and 3000 ms inclusive are used to calculate the mean RT for each condition (N = 1, 2, 4, 8). Convert N to Information Entropy (H = log2(N)) and fit a simple linear regression for each valid participant (Mean_RT = Intercept + Slope * H) to determine the baseline motor time (intercept) and information processing rate (slope). Compute the group mean slope and group mean intercept across all valid participants. Save a JSON report to ~/pebl/analysis/hicks_report.json. The report must contain the group means and a 'participants' list. Valid participant entries must include the keys 'id', 'mean_rt_by_n', 'hicks_slope_ms_per_bit', and 'hicks_intercept_ms'. Excluded participant entries must include 'id', 'excluded', and 'reason'.

metadata.contamination_description: overall accuracy < 60% due to button mashing
